title: Early Americas
description: Practice your skills with a quiz on early Americas!

assessment_items.question_content:
[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ image 2]]

**Items such as the ones shown above are best understood in the context of what development in early American civilizations?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: Image of ruins of Chavin de Huantar.
options.caption: Image of ruins of Chavin de Huantar. Image credit: [Wikimedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chav%C3%ADn_culture#/media/File:Chavin_-_Ao%C3%BBt_2007.jpg)
Image of Olmec colossal head. Image credit: [Wikimedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Olmec_colossal_heads#/media/File:Olmec_warrior.jpg)

choices.content: The use of monumental art and architecture for religious and political purposes
The advent of written record-keeping to organize building projects
The spread and mixing of cultures through interregional trade
Advances in urban planning to incorporate monumental art

assessment_items.question_content:
[[☃ image 3]]

[[☃ image 4]]

**Based on these images, which of the following was most likely a __similarity__ between Teotihuacan and Cahokia?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: Image of the Pyramid of the Moon, Teotihuacan
options.caption: Image of the Pyramid of the Moon, Teotihuacan. Image credit: [Wikimedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pyramid_of_the_Moon#/media/File:Piramide_de_la_Luna_072006.jpg)
Image of Monks Mound, Cahokia. Image credit: [Wikimedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Monks_Mound#/media/File:Monks_Mound_in_July.JPG)

choices.content: Both existed at the same time
Both were capitals of large empires
Both had highly specialized economies
Both relied on agricultural surpluses

assessment_items.question_content:
[[☃ image 1]] 

[[☃ image 2]]


**The sites shown above most directly contributed to which of the following in the Americas?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: Image of the Pyramid of the Moon, Teotihuacan
options.caption: Image of the Pyramid of the Moon, Teotihuacan. Image credit: [Wikimedia](https://cdn.kastatic.org/ka-perseus-images/1b6fc5b5c219b489291d0c29bd9390e9f37a9603.jpg)
Image of Monks Mound, Cahokia. Image credit: [Wikimedia](https://cdn.kastatic.org/ka-perseus-images/21fdb8529d3c02dbc637c6b895ab0f19d9833f8a.jpg)

choices.content: A sense of shared culture due to common religious ritual
Increased disputes due to religious disagreements
The decline of civilizations due to environmental damage
The use of monumental architecture for military defense

assessment_items.question_content:
[[☃ image 3]]

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ image 2]]


**Taken together, the images above best represents what __similar__ development among most early civilizations?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.caption: From Ancient South America. Image credit: [Wikimedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Quipu#/media/File:Quipu.png)

options.alt: Inscribed clay tablet from Mesopotamia, c. 2052 B.C.E.
options.caption:
From Ancient Mesopotamia, c. 2052 B.C.E.

Image credit: [LACMA](https://collections.lacma.org/node/228141)

choices.content: Record-keeping systems
Cuneiform writing
Distinct artistic styles
Epic story-telling

assessment_items.question_content:
[[☃ image 1]]

**The pattern of Olmec influence shown in the map was most likely the result of which of the following Olmec actions in Mesoamerica?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: Map of Olmec heartland (in orange) and sites showing Olmec influences (in yellow)
options.caption: Map of Olmec area of political control (in orange) and sites outside the Olmec heartland where Olmec cultural influence has been found in the form of artifacts (in yellow). Image credit: [Wikimedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Olmec#/media/File:Formative_Era_sites.svg)

choices.content: They were involved in trade networks with other communities
They sent religious missionaries to spread Olmec beliefs
They conquered much of the region though military means
They created colonies outside the Olmec heartland

assessment_items.question_content:
[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ image 2]]

**The construction of sites such as those shown above could __best__ be used as evidence for which of the following in the Americas?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: Image of the Pyramid of the Moon, Teotihuacan
options.caption: Image of the Pyramid of the Moon, Teotihuacan. Image credit: [Wikimedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pyramid_of_the_Moon#/media/File:Piramide_de_la_Luna_072006.jpg)
Image of Monks Mound, Cahokia. Image credit: [Wikimedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Monks_Mound#/media/File:Monks_Mound_in_July.JPG)

choices.content: The emergence of centralized authority
Syncretism, or blending, of cultures
Development of distinct merchant classes
The use of enslaved labor for public works

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the passage below and answer the question:

>A city provides goods and services for its surrounding area, exchanging food from the countryside for the products of its sophisticated craftspeople . . . Cahokia . . . was a huge collection of farmers packed in tightly. It had few specialized craftworkers and no middle-class merchants.  

From Charles Mann, _1491_

**This passage could best be used to make what kind of comparison or contrast between Cahokia and other early civilizations in the Americas?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: To provide a contrast with other cities in the early Americas
To illustrate common features of most early American cities
To show widespread specialization of labor common in the early Americas
To contrast the range of trade networks in the early Americas

assessment_items.question_content:
[[☃ image 1]]

**The action depicted in the image above best illustrates which of the following _similarities_ between early states?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: Image of a Maya king installing subordinate rulers
options.caption: Image of a Maya king installing subordinate rulers. Image credit: [Wikimedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ancient_Maya_art#/media/File:Cancuenpanel3.jpg)

choices.content: The existence of social hierarchies
The enforcement of filial piety
The use of enslaved subordinate labor
The belief in divine kingship

assessment_items.question_content:
[[☃ image 1]]

**The kind of political and social hierarchy depicted in the image above _differed_ most strongly from which of the following?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: Nobles presenting captives as tribute to the Maya Lord Chan Muwan. Nobles wear animal headgear, and Chan Muwan's wife is pictured, third from the right. Bonampak, Mexico c. 790 B.C.E.
options.caption: This image depicts nobles presenting captives as tribute to Mayan Lord Chan Muwan (top center). Nobles wear animal headgear, and Chan Muwan's wife is featured, third from the right. Bonampak, Mexico c. 790 B.C.E. Image credit: [LatinAmericanStudies.org](http://www.latinamericanstudies.org/bonampak/Room-2_3.gif).

choices.content: Divine kingship in ancient Egypt
Autocratic rule in Achaemenid Persia
Democratic structures in classical Athens
Tribute rituals in ancient China

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the passage below and answer the question:

>A city provides goods and services for its surrounding area, exchanging food from the countryside for the products of its sophisticated craftspeople . . . Cahokia . . . was a huge collection of farmers packed in tightly. It had few specialized craftworkers and no middle-class merchants.  

From Charles Mann, _1491_

**Although we don't know the exact political structure of Cahokia, which of the following facts could best serve as evidence for the argument that Cahokia was _not_ a centralized state?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Cahokia's economy had little complexity
Cahokia relied on agricultural surplus to survive
Cahokia provided goods to surrounding regions
Cahokia had a large population

assessment_items.question_content:
[[☃ image 1]]

**This image could best be used as evidence for which of the following similarities between states in the classical period?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: Nobles presenting captives as tribute to the Maya Lord Chan Muwan. Nobles wear animal headgear, and Chan Muwan's wife is pictured, third from the right. Bonampak, Mexico c. 790 B.C.E.
options.caption: This image depicts nobles presenting captives as tribute to Mayan Lord Chan Muwan (top center). Nobles wear animal headgear, and Chan Muwan's wife is featured, third from the right. Bonampak, Mexico c. 790 B.C.E. Image credit: [LatinAmericanStudies.org](http://www.latinamericanstudies.org/bonampak/Room-2_3.gif).

choices.content: Rulers often required social elites to prove their loyalty through tributes
Rulers often placed male elites at equal standing with noble women
Rulers were often required to publicly redistribute their personal wealth
Rulers were often worshipped as divine beings in public ceremonies
